parentQuestion: A patient cannot smile, raise their eyebrows, or close their eye tightly on the ...

scaffolds.q: Smiling, frowning, and raising eyebrows all use facial muscles.
scaffolds.explain: Facial expressions are produced by muscles of facial expression — small muscles that attach to the skin and move it to create expressions. they're all controlled by one nerve: CN VII. All of human emotion displayed through one cable.

scaffolds.q: The same nerve controls all muscles of facial expression on one side.
scaffolds.explain: The facial nerve (CN VII) provides motor innervation to all muscles of facial expression on one side of the face. Consider the elegance here:the vulnerability — lose it, and half your face stops moving.
